title: senecio crenulatus
intro: Senecio crenulatus, also known as New Zealand fireweed or parrot's beak, is a flowering plant species native to New Zealand. This plant contains pyrrolizidine alkaloids, which have been found to have medicinal properties, although they also have potential toxicity and should be used with caution. The medicinal properties of Senecio crenulatus have been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, respiratory problems, and skin conditions, although its effectiveness is largely anecdotal. In herbal preparations, Senecio crenulatus is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, but its use should be strictly regulated and under the guidance of a qualified healthcare professional due to its potential toxicity.
